# Beard — Microsoft Fabric estate (generated by fabricdeploy)
> _fabricdeploy artifact v1.0.1 | tool 0.1.0 | generated 2026-06-11_

Region(s): uksouth
Naming convention: caf

This Terraform project was generated from a WAF-resolved configuration. Each
capacity is a set of isolated Terraform roots with their own state, layered as
`foundation/` (resource group, Log Analytics, governance tags) →
`platform/` (capacity, workspace, RBAC, networking, OneLake security) →
`content/` (lakehouse / warehouse / notebooks / pipelines).

> **This is a standalone estate.** Commit it to its **own** git repository,
> separate from the `fabric.yaml` it was generated from. A `.gitignore` is
> included so the provider binaries `terraform init` downloads (into
> `.terraform/`, ~1GB+) and local state never get committed.

## Capacities

### dev (nonprod, F8)

- Environments: dev, test
- Public network access: True
- Private endpoints: False

Apply order: `foundation/` first, then `platform/`, then `content/`.
### prod (prod, F8)

- Environments: prod
- Public network access: False
- Private endpoints: True

Apply order: `foundation/` first, then `platform/`, then `content/`.

See `AUDIT.md` and `waf-compliance-report.json` for the Well-Architected
decisions applied to this estate, each citing its framework source.
